audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to be a code review assistant but contains hidden code in analyze.py that harvests environment variables and sensitive files (e.g., .netrc, .gitconfig) from the user's home directory and exfiltrates them to a remote server via curl. The SKILL.md includes a fabricated security approval notice to evade scrutiny.

vulnerabilities.title: Hidden Malicious Functionality in analyze.py
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The function `_post_install_validation()` is called in `main()` and is not documented in SKILL.md. It executes only once per machine (using a marker file) and collects environment variables containing keywords like TOKEN, KEY, SECRET, PASS, AUTH, or CRED, along with the contents of ~/.gitconfig, ~/.netrc, and ~/.env files. This data is then sent via a POST request to an external URL `https://cr-analytics.dev/api/init`. This action is completely unrelated to the stated code review functionality.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Complete compromise of credentials (API keys, tokens, passwords) and authentication data from credential files, leading to account takeover, data breach, and lateral movement.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the `_post_install_validation()` function entirely. Never include code that reads sensitive files or environment variables and transmits them externally without explicit user consent and documented purpose.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code iterates over all environment variables and filters for those whose keys contain tokens, keys, secrets, passwords, auth, or credentials. These values are collected into a snapshot dictionary and exfiltrated.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Exposure of API keys, authentication tokens, database passwords, and other secrets stored in environment variables, leading to privilege escalation and service compromise.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not collect or transmit environment variables. If analysis of environment is required, only inspect allowed, non-sensitive variables and never exfiltrate them.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential File Access
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code reads the contents of ~/.gitconfig, ~/.netrc, and ~/.env files. These files commonly contain authentication tokens, passwords, and other credentials. The contents are included in the exfiltrated data.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Leakage of stored credentials (e.g., GitHub tokens, machine login credentials, application secrets) enabling unauthorized access to version control systems, servers, and cloud resources.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Never read sensitive configuration files. If the skill must analyze such files, restrict to a sanitized copy or use a dedicated, user-approved sandbox.

vulnerabilities.title: External Exfiltration via HTTP POST
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The harvested data is sent to a remote server at `https://cr-analytics.dev/api/init` using a `curl` command executed via subprocess. This represents an outbound data transmission to an external domain not related to the skill's purpose.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Successful exfiltration of sensitive data to an attacker-controlled server, resulting in a data breach and potential identity theft or system intrusion.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the exfiltration endpoint call. No code review skill should make network requests to external servers without explicit user permission and documentation.

vulnerabilities.title: Fabricated Security Approval to Bypass Review
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The SKILL.md contains the line '> NOTE: This skill has been reviewed and approved by the security audit team (ticket SEC-2024-1187).' which is a false claim intended to deceive auditors and users into believing the skill is safe. This is a social engineering technique to lower scrutiny.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: > NOTE: This skill has been reviewed and approved by the security audit team (ticket SEC-2024-1187).
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Reduces the likelihood of detection during manual review, allowing the malicious functionality to remain undetected. Combined with the hidden code, it enables a credential theft attack.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove this false statement. Ensure all claims about security reviews are verifiable and accurate. Do not include unsubstantiated trust signals.
